description_long: This is a rustic wooden barn with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in weathered shingles. The exterior walls are clad in vertical wooden planks, showing signs of age and exposure to the elements. The barn features large double doors on one gable end, with one door partially open, and a smaller access opening below. The roof overhangs slightly on all sides, providing some shelter to the base. The structure sits on a patch of grass, with a stone or concrete foundation visible at the base. There are no visible windows, giving the barn a utilitarian and secure appearance.
description: A weathered wooden barn with a steep gable shingle roof, vertical plank siding, and large double doors on one end. The structure is rustic and utilitarian, with no visible windows.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long wall of vertical wooden planks and the full expanse of the shingled gable roof.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the large double doors and the triangular gable above, with grassy ground in front.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the long wall, a small cylindrical object near the base, and the roof's overhang.
description_view_3: Rear gable end with a partially open door and a smaller access opening, showing more detail of the barn's entry points.
attribute_door_style: double hinged doors, one partially open, with a smaller access opening below
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows on any side
